summary: For 4D image resize kernels, a row-based totalRows scheduler can still produce widespread correctness mismatches even when the nearest-index formula is right. Rewrite tiling and kernel traversal to split work by flattened N*C planes with explicit workOffset/localWork, following the verified image-op pattern.

If nearest-neighbor x4 upsample still fails after forcing single-core execution, the bug is not just blockDim selection. Replace rowIdx-to-plane address derivation with a plane-wise loop over N*C and compute input/output row bases inside each plane to align device memory traversal with known-good resize kernels.
